operations.notes: Create an exception for unified security policies.

responseMessages.message: The specified network item is not valid.
The specified zone name does not exist.
The security policy with the specified name does not exist
The security policy 'created by' user is not valid.
The security policy with the specified name already exists.
Network Object of device id =|device_id| , with uid = |domain_id| does not exist
The from zone name = |from_zone_name| does not exist
The to zone name = |to_zone_name| does not exist
The security policy name = |security_policy_name| does not exist
The security policy 'created by' user = |user_name| is not valid
The security policy exception cannot be created
Not permitted to access this context.
The user does not have permission to this action.

operations.notes:
Gets a list of all unified security policy exceptions.
If you have TOS Aurora installed, and you are using the SecureTrack REST API to get USP exceptions, only exceptions that were created using the API will be returned and that do not include network objects. USP Traffic Exceptions created in USP Exceptions Viewer, or GraphQL will not be returned.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Starting page for query allowable type is numeric

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: Number of pages for query starting from starting page allowable type in numeric

responseMessages.message: Not permitted to access this context.

operations.notes:
Gets a specific exception for unified security policies.
If you have TOS Aurora installed, and you are using the SecureTrack REST API to get USP exceptions, only exceptions that were created using the API will be returned and that do not include network objects. USP Traffic Exceptions created in USP Exceptions Viewer, or GraphQL will not be returned.

parameters.name: exceptionId
parameters.description: The ID of the exception to get.

responseMessages.message: Not permitted to access this context.
A security policy exception with the specified ID was not found.

operations.notes:
Delete the specific unified security policy exceptions.
If you have TOS Aurora installed, and you are using the SecureTrack REST API to get USP exceptions, only exceptions created using the API will be returned. USP Traffic Exceptions created in USP Exceptions Viewer, or GraphQL will not be returned.

parameters.name: exceptionId
parameters.description: The ID of the exception to delete.

responseMessages.message: The security policy with the specified ID does not exist
Not permitted to access this context.

operations.summary: Start a task to calculate matching rules for an exception
operations.notes:
Create a calculation task to find matching rules.
The results will be on the relevant devices for each access.
Returns a task Id that can be used to check the task status and get the task results

parameters.name: exceptionId
parameters.description: Security policy Exception ID on which to run the API.

responseMessages.message: Not permitted to Security policy exceptions.
A security policy exception with the specified ID was not found.

parameters.name: taskId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the matching rules task.

responseMessages.message: Matching rules task has been aborted successfully.
Not permitted to Security policy exceptions.
Matching rules task was not found.

parameters.name: taskId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the matching rules task.

responseMessages.message: Matching rules task results were received.
Not permitted to Security policy exceptions.
An error occurred during the calculation.
The calculation for the task has not finished yet.

parameters.name: taskId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the matching rules task.

responseMessages.message: Matching rules task status was received.
Not permitted to Security policy exceptions.
Matching rules task status was not found.
